sections.title: Slide 3: Who are we and why are we here?

elements.text: Who are we and why are we here?

content.text: NASA Earth Science Division:
providing decades of data to help in understanding our planet’s interconnected systems, from a global scale down to minute processes.
NASA produces data that is free to access & use for everyone!
You can discover decades of data for anywhere in the world!
NASA offers tools & services for viewing, accessing, & interacting with data!
NASA has over
unique products & over
petabytes of Earth science data!

elements.alt: Earth with clouds
Orange Earth map
Ocean and islands

content.text: Interactively browse ~1,000 global, full-resolution satellite imagery layers, known as the Global Imagery Browse Services (GIBS)
Discover imagery within minutes to hours of it being acquired
as well as imagery from the
past few decades
Catalog accessible via time-enabled OGC WMS end point

Worldview side-by-side swipe comparison showing fires and thermal anomalies

content.text: Events:
Users can explore recent events
Animation:
Users can create animations & export to imagery as GIFs.
Comparison:
Users can compare imagery side by side using a swipe tool.

content.text: Earthdata Search provides
discovery, filtering, visualization & access
to all of NASA’s Earth Science data.
This includes
thousands
of data collections.
~1400 collections are now
customizable

elements.alt: QR Code
Earthdata Search showing search results for 'Air Quality' with matching collections like OMI/Aura Multi-wavelength Aerosol Optical Depth

content.text: The platform offers free-text search & filtering by science area (keyword), platform, and/or instrument.
Filter by date/time & spatial region

Red box

Screenshot showing 'Projection Options' dialog with 'Re-projection Options' dropdown. 'Geographic' is currently selected, with other options being 'No Change', 'Universal Transverse Mercator', and 'Polar Stereographic'.

sections.title: Earthdata GIS: GIS Services, Maps, & Apps

elements.alt: Screenshot of Earthdata GIS Featured Layers page showing various data layers like U.S. Social Vulnerability Index Grid, Annual Global High-Resolution Ext..., Yale Center for Earth Observation (..., Tropospheric Emissions: Monitorin..., Building Thermal & Thermal Moist..., 4-Year Rolling Building Thermal & T..., Global Man made l...
QR Code linking to Earthdata GIS.
Diagram showing EGIS (Earthdata GIS) at the center connecting to various tools and platforms like Jupyter, OGC, R, GDAL, and QGIS.

content.text: Earthdata GIS is a resource for distributing cloud-native, GIS-ready NASA Earth Science data, services, & resources. These include ArcGIS & Open Geospatial Consortium (OGC)-compliant raster & feature geospatial services.
175 image & 25 feature services offered!

sections.title: NASA AGOL: Integrate NASA GIS Data

Graphic titled 'GIS at NASA' showing 'Geographic Information Systems - going beyond mapping! Providing powerful capabilities to visualize, analyze, and interact with big data' over an image of a satellite observing Earth.

content.text: NASA has lots of data, layers, & maps available in the ArcGIS Online Platform,
ready to be integrated
into your next project or study.

sections.title: The ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

elements.alt: Screenshot of the ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World website with a search for 'NASA Earthdata'. Results show 'Gridded Population of the World, Version 4 (GPWv4): Pop...' and 'Low Elevation Coastal Zones derived from MERIT-DEM'.

content.text: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World is the foremost collection of geographic information from around the globe. It includes maps, apps, and data layers to support your work.
< We are working to continually to increase the number of
NASA layers available in the Living Atlas of the World
Gridded Population of the World, Version 4 (GPWv4): Pop...
Image Service By NASA_Earthdata

Gridded Population of the World, Version 4 (GPWv4): Population Density, Revision 11. Published on: 2018-12-31T00:00:00.000Z. Created by Center for International Earth Science Information Network - CIESIN - Columbia University. Published by NASA Socioeconomic Data and Applications Center (SEDAC).
Low Elevation Coastal Zones derived from MERIT-DEM
Image Service By NASA_Earthdata

Low Elevation Coastal Zone (LECZ) Urban-Rural Population and Land Area Estimates, Version 3. Published on: 2021-12-14. Created by Center for International Earth Science Information Network - CIESIN - Columbia University, and CUNY Institute for Demographic Research - CIDR - City University of New...

content.text: In an interagency collaborative effort, the DRCS supported mapping of landslides using optical imagery, with locations of landslides mapped on the USGS Landslide Dashboard

The Earthdata

has videos specific to the GIS-minded users.
